config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas orang tertentu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar seseorang seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati sekaligus tanggung jawab sosial dijalankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual; the two have to be balanced, and individual rights need real protection. From my experience in China, many people value social stability and collective welfare, and that can be important for public order and long-term development, but it can also become a problem if it is used to silence personal freedom or excuse unfair treatment. A society is stronger when people feel protected, not when they are told their rights can be ignored whenever the majority says so. To me, the real priority should be clear limits: society can ask for responsibility, but it should not override basic individual rights without strong justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang adil adalah mencari keseimbangan dengan perlindungan hak dasar yang jelas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama soal ketertiban, kesehatan, dan bencana, tetapi itu tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menginjak kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika negara dan mayoritas mau dibatasi oleh hak individu, bukan sebaliknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule, because in practice that idea is too easy to abuse. From my perspective in China, people do often value harmony, stability, and collective goals, and those things are real, but I have also seen how quickly “for the common good” can become a blanket excuse to ignore fairness or silence inconvenient voices. A society is not truly strong if individuals feel they have no protection when the majority or the state claims necessity. The better principle is that society can ask for responsibility, but basic rights should remain the default limit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang berlebihan, apalagi kalau kontrol dan akuntabilitasnya lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk ketertiban, kesehatan, dan keadaan darurat, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang membiarkan mayoritas menekan minoritas, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan tanpa menghapus martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general principle, because that rule is too easy to use as a justification for abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize harmony, stability, and collective goals, since those values can help a country function well, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or ignore unfair treatment. Public interests matter in emergencies and in issues like health and order, yet basic rights should remain the default limit, not something the majority can override whenever it wants. A strong society is one that protects people first, because people will only genuinely support the collective when they feel their dignity and rights are secure.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
